{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 林忠満 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[安土桃山時代]] - [[江戸時代]]前期 | 生誕 = [[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]） | 死没 = [[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]） | 改名 = 竹童丸、頼次 | 別名 = 助十郎、惣右衛門 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = 日輝 | 霊名 = | 墓所 = [[池上本門寺]] | 官位 = 伊予守、摂津守 | 幕府 = 江戸幕府 | 主君 = 豊臣秀吉 | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[能勢氏]] | 父母 = [[能勢頼幸]]、[[波多野秀親]]の娘 | 兄弟 = [[能勢頼道|頼道]]、[[能勢頼郡|頼郡]]、'''頼次'''、金剛院、[[安威源秀]]室、[[井上氏友]]室、女子2 | 妻 = [[井上氏幸]]の娘 | 子 = '''[[能勢頼重|頼重]]'''、[[能勢頼隆|頼隆]]、[[能勢頼之|頼之]]、[[能勢頼永|頼永]]、[[能勢頼平|頼平]]、[[田村顕当]]室、[[近江局]]、[[織田高重]]室、''[[太田資次]]室'' | 特記事項 = }}
'''能勢 頼次'''（のせ よりつぐ）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期の[[武将]]。
== 生涯 == [[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕える。[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を監視して家康に伝えた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、ここで[[塩川氏]]の残党を滅ぼした。塩川氏はかつて長兄[[能勢頼道|頼道]]を討った仇であった。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。
== 出典 == * 『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻第272
{{DEFAULTSORT:のせ よりつく}} [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:江戸幕府旗本]] [[Category:1562年生]] [[Category:1626年没]]

'''能勢 頼次'''（のせ よりつぐ）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期の[[武将]]。
== 生涯 == [[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕える。[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を監視して家康に伝えた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、ここで[[塩川氏]]の残党を滅ぼした。塩川氏はかつて長兄[[能勢頼道|頼道]]を討った仇であった。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。

== 生涯 == [[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕える。[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を監視して家康に伝えた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、ここで[[塩川氏]]の残党を滅ぼした。塩川氏はかつて長兄[[能勢頼道|頼道]]を討った仇であった。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。

'''能勢 頼次'''（のせ よりつぐ）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期の[[武将]]・[[江戸幕府]][[旗本]]。
== 生涯 == [[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕える。[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を監視して家康に伝えた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、ここで[[塩川氏]]の残党を滅ぼした。塩川氏はかつて長兄[[能勢頼道|頼道]]を討った仇であった。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。
== 出典 == * 『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻第272

{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 能勢頼次 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[安土桃山時代]] - [[江戸時代]]前期 | 生誕 = [[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]） | 死没 = [[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]） | 改名 = 竹童丸、頼次 | 別名 = 助十郎、惣右衛門 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = 日輝 | 霊名 = | 墓所 = [[池上本門寺]] | 官位 = 伊予守、摂津守 | 幕府 = 江戸幕府 | 主君 = [[豊臣秀吉]]→[[徳川家康]]→[[徳川秀忠|秀忠]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[能勢氏]] | 父母 = [[能勢頼幸]]、[[波多野秀親]]の娘 | 兄弟 = [[能勢頼道|頼道]]、[[能勢頼郡|頼郡]]、'''頼次'''、金剛院、[[安威源秀]]室、[[井上氏友]]室、女子2 | 妻 = [[井上氏幸]]の娘 | 子 = '''[[能勢頼重|頼重]]'''、[[能勢頼隆|頼隆]]、[[能勢頼之|頼之]]、[[能勢頼永|頼永]]、[[能勢頼平|頼平]]、娘（[[田村顕当]]室→[[松平昌重]]室）、[[近江局]]、娘（[[織田高重]]室）、''養女（松平昌重の娘、[[太田資次]]室）'' | 特記事項 = }}
'''能勢 頼次'''（のせ よりつぐ）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期の[[武将]]・[[江戸幕府]][[旗本]]。
== 生涯 == [[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。 [[山崎の戦い]]で[[明智光秀]]に味方し敗戦後に逃亡し[[塩川長満]]に領地を奪われた。改めて[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕えることに成功したが旧領は回復されなかったらしく、『能勢町史』によると九州征伐後の能勢郡は[[島津義弘]]が在京賄料として拝領していた。 秀吉の死後には[[徳川家康]]に近づき、[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際は、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を監視して家康に伝えた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。[[野間神社]]の再興と[[地黄城]]の築城に着手した。 慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、ここで[[塩川氏]]の残党を滅ぼした。塩川氏はかつて長兄[[能勢頼道|頼道]]を討った仇であった。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。
[[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]）[[江戸]]で没した。幕府は[[遺言]]によって領地をその子孫に分与した。長男[[能勢頼重]]に3,000石、次男[[能勢頼高]]に1,500石、三男[[能勢頼之（安土桃山時代）|能勢頼之]]に1,000石、四男[[能勢頼永]]に846石、五男[[能勢頼平]]300石をそれぞれに与えた。地黄城は頼重が引き継ぎ後の11代目[[能勢頼富]]まで続いた。
[[弓 (武器)|弓]]術に長けて知勇に優れ、明智光秀に味方して一度は領地を失ったものの時勢を見分けて旧領回復に成功し、善政を敷いて繁栄を招いたことで能勢氏中興の祖と言われている。
== 出典 == * 『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻第272
{{DEFAULTSORT:のせ よりつく}} [[Category:能勢氏|よりつく]] [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:江戸幕府旗本]] [[Category:1562年生]] [[Category:1626年没]]

'''能勢 頼次'''（のせ よりつぐ）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期の[[武将]]・[[江戸幕府]][[旗本]]。
== 生涯 == [[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）、[[摂津国]][[能勢郡]]の[[国人]]・[[能勢頼幸]]の子として誕生。
[[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。
[[山崎の戦い]]で[[明智光秀]]に味方するも、敗戦後に逃亡し[[塩川長満]]に領地を奪われた。光秀を破った[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕えたが旧領は回復されなかったらしく、『能勢町史』によると[[九州平定|九州征伐]]後の能勢郡は[[島津義弘]]が在京賄料として拝領していた。
秀吉の死後には[[徳川家康]]に近づき、[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際は、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を監視して家康に伝えた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。[[野間神社]]の再興と[[地黄城]]の築城に着手した。
慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、ここで[[塩川氏]]の残党を滅ぼした。塩川氏はかつて長兄[[能勢頼道|頼道]]を討った仇であった。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。
[[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]）[[江戸]]で没した。幕府は[[遺言]]によって領地をその子孫に分与した。長男[[能勢頼重]]に3,000石、次男[[能勢頼高]]に1,500石、三男[[能勢頼之（安土桃山時代）|能勢頼之]]に1,000石、四男[[能勢頼永]]に846石、五男[[能勢頼平]]300石をそれぞれに与えた。地黄城は頼重が引き継ぎ後の11代目[[能勢頼富]]まで続いた。
[[弓 (武器)|弓]]術に長けて知勇に優れ、明智光秀に味方して一度は領地を失ったものの時勢を見分けて旧領回復に成功し、善政を敷いて繁栄を招いたことで能勢氏中興の祖と言われている。

'''能勢 頼次'''（のせ よりつぐ）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期の[[武将]]・[[江戸幕府]][[旗本]]。
== 生涯 == [[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）、[[摂津国]][[能勢郡]]の[[国人]]・[[能勢頼幸]]の子として誕生。
[[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。
[[山崎の戦い]]で[[明智光秀]]に味方するも、敗戦後に逃亡し[[塩川長満]]に領地を奪われた。光秀を破った[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕えたが旧領は回復されなかったらしく、『能勢町史』によると[[九州平定|九州征伐]]後の能勢郡は、[[島津義弘]]が在京賄料として拝領していた。
秀吉の死後は[[徳川家康]]に近づき、[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）に[[石田三成]]が家康を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際は頼次の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、頼次もその動向を監視して家康に伝えた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従いその功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。[[野間神社]]の再興と[[地黄城]]の築城に着手した。
慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口の守備にあたった。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、ここで[[塩川氏]]の残党を滅ぼした。塩川氏はかつて長兄の[[能勢頼道|頼道]]を討った仇であった。戦後は2300石の加増を受け、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男の[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居した。
[[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]）、頼次は[[江戸]]で没した。幕府は頼次の遺言によって領地をその子孫に分与した。嫡男の[[能勢頼重]]に3,000石、次男の[[能勢頼高]]に1,500石、三男の[[能勢頼之（安土桃山時代）|能勢頼之]]に1,000石、四男の[[能勢頼永]]に846石、五男の[[能勢頼平]]300石をそれぞれに与えた。地黄城は頼重が引き継ぎ後の11代目[[能勢頼富]]まで続いた。
頼次は[[弓 (武器)|弓]]術に長けて知勇に優れ、光秀に味方して一度は領地を失ったものの時勢を見分けて旧領回復に成功し、善政を敷いて繁栄を招いたことで能勢氏中興の祖と言われている。
== 出典 == * 『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻第272

{{出典の明記|date=2023年3月}} {{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 能勢頼次 | 画像 = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | 画像サイズ = 250px | 画像説明 = 能勢頼次画像/[[清普寺]]所蔵 | 時代 = [[安土桃山時代]] - [[江戸時代]]前期 | 生誕 = [[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]） | 死没 = [[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]） | 改名 = 竹童丸（[[幼名]]）、頼次 | 別名 = 助十郎、惣右衛門 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = 日輝 | 霊名 = | 墓所 = [[池上本門寺]] | 官位 = [[伊予国|伊予守]]、[[摂津国|摂津守]] | 幕府 = [[江戸幕府]][[旗本]] | 主君 = [[明智光秀]]→[[豊臣秀吉]]→[[豊臣秀頼|秀頼]]→[[徳川家康]]→[[徳川秀忠|秀忠]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[能勢氏]] | 父母 = [[能勢頼幸]]、[[波多野秀親]]の娘 | 兄弟 = [[能勢頼道|頼道]]、[[能勢頼郡|頼郡]]、'''頼次'''、金剛院、[[安威源秀]]室、[[井上氏友]]室、女子2 | 妻 = [[井上氏幸]]の娘 | 子 = '''[[能勢頼重|頼重]]'''、[[能勢頼隆|頼隆]]、[[能勢頼之|頼之]]、[[能勢頼永|頼永]]、[[能勢頼平|頼平]]、娘（[[田村顕当]]室→[[松平昌重]]室）、[[近江局]]、娘（[[織田高重]]室）、''養女（松平昌重の娘、[[太田資次]]室）'' | 特記事項 = }}
'''能勢 頼次'''（のせ よりつぐ）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期の[[武将]]・[[江戸幕府]][[旗本]]。
== 生涯 == [[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）、[[摂津国]][[能勢郡]]の[[国人]]・[[能勢頼幸]]の子として誕生。
[[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。
[[山崎の戦い]]で[[明智光秀]]に味方するも、敗戦後に逃亡し[[塩川長満]]に領地を奪われた。光秀を破った[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕えたが旧領は当然回復されなかったらしく、『能勢町史』によると[[九州平定|九州征伐]]後の能勢郡は[[島津義弘]]が在京賄料として拝領していた。
秀吉の死後には[[徳川家康]]に近づき、[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際は、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を家康に密告し続けた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。[[野間神社]]の再興と[[地黄城]]の築城に着手した。
慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、どさくさに紛れ[[塩川氏]]を滅ぼした。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。
[[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]）[[江戸]]で没した。幕府は[[遺言]]によって領地をその子孫に分与した。長男[[能勢頼重]]に3,000石、次男[[能勢頼高]]に1,500石、三男[[能勢頼之（安土桃山時代）|能勢頼之]]に1,000石、四男[[能勢頼永]]に846石、五男[[能勢頼平]]300石をそれぞれに与えた。地黄城は頼重が引き継ぎ後の11代目[[能勢頼富]]まで続いた。
== 出典 == * 『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻第272

{{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺}} | period = [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜}}*, Volume 272
[[Category:Nose clan]] [[Category:Samurai of the Sengoku period]] [[Category:Hatamoto]] [[Category:1562 births]] [[Category:1626 deaths]] [[ja:能勢頼次]]

'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜}}*, Volume 272

{{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺}} | period = [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜}}*, Volume 272

{{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺}} | period = [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜}}*, Volume 272

Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. {{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺}} | period = [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜}}*, Volume 272

Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. {{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺}} | period = [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜}}*, Volume 272

'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜}}*, Volume 272

{{出典の明記|date=2023年3月}} {{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 能勢頼次 | 画像 = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | 画像サイズ = 250px | 画像説明 = 能勢頼次画像/[[清普寺]]所蔵 | 時代 = [[安土桃山時代]] - [[江戸時代]]前期 | 生誕 = [[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]） | 死没 = [[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]） | 改名 = 竹童丸（[[幼名]]）、頼次 | 別名 = 助十郎、惣右衛門 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = 日輝 | 霊名 = | 墓所 = [[池上本門寺]] | 官位 = [[伊予国|伊予守]]、[[摂津国|摂津守]] | 幕府 = [[江戸幕府]][[旗本]] | 主君 = [[明智光秀]]→[[豊臣秀吉]]→[[豊臣秀頼|秀頼]]→[[徳川家康]]→[[徳川秀忠|秀忠]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[能勢氏]] | 父母 = [[能勢頼幸]]、[[波多野秀親]]の娘 | 兄弟 = [[能勢頼道|頼道]]、[[能勢頼郡|頼郡]]、'''頼次'''、金剛院、[[安威源秀]]室、[[井上氏友]]室、女子2 | 妻 = [[井上氏幸]]の娘 | 子 = '''[[能勢頼重|頼重]]'''、[[能勢頼隆|頼隆]]、[[能勢頼之|頼之]]、[[能勢頼永|頼永]]、[[能勢頼平|頼平]]、娘（[[田村顕当]]室→[[松平昌重]]室）、[[近江局]]、娘（[[織田高重]]室）、''養女（松平昌重の娘、[[太田資次]]室）'' | 特記事項 = }}
'''能勢 頼次'''（のせ よりつぐ）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期の[[武将]]・[[江戸幕府]][[旗本]]。
== 生涯 == [[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）、[[摂津国]][[能勢郡]]の[[国人]]・[[能勢頼幸]]の子として誕生。
[[能勢氏]]は代々[[足利氏|足利将軍家]]に仕えたが、[[織田信長]]の時代に兄二人はいずれも戦死していた。
[[山崎の戦い]]で[[明智光秀]]に味方するも、敗戦後に逃亡し[[塩川長満]]に領地を奪われた。光秀を破った[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕えたが旧領は当然回復されなかったらしく、『能勢町史』によると[[九州平定|九州征伐]]後の能勢郡は[[島津義弘]]が在京賄料として拝領していた。
秀吉の死後には[[徳川家康]]に近づき、[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際は、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を家康に密告し続けた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。[[野間神社]]の再興と[[地黄城]]の築城に着手した。
慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、どさくさに紛れ[[塩川氏]]を滅ぼした。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。
[[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]）[[江戸]]で没した。幕府は[[遺言]]によって領地をその子孫に分与した。長男[[能勢頼重]]に3,000石、次男[[能勢頼高]]に1,500石、三男[[能勢頼之（安土桃山時代）|能勢頼之]]に1,000石、四男[[能勢頼永]]に846石、五男[[能勢頼平]]300石をそれぞれに与えた。地黄城は頼重が引き継ぎ後の11代目[[能勢頼富]]まで続いた。
== 出典 == * 『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻第272

[[山崎の戦い]]で[[明智光秀]]に味方するも、敗戦後に逃亡し[[塩川長満]]に領地を奪われた。光秀を破った[[豊臣秀吉]]に仕えたが旧領は当然回復されなかったらしく、『能勢町史』によると[[九州平定|九州征伐]]後の能勢郡は[[島津義弘]]が在京賄料として拝領していた。
秀吉の死後には[[徳川家康]]に近づき、[[慶長]]4年（[[1599年]]）[[石田三成]]が[[徳川家康]]を襲撃するという巷説が流れた際は、自身の屋敷が三成の屋敷の隣であったため、その動向を家康に密告し続けた。慶長5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では家康に従い、その功で[[能勢郡]]3000石を与えられ、また同地に6800石を預けられる。[[野間神社]]の再興と[[地黄城]]の築城に着手した。
慶長19年（[[1614年]]）[[大坂の陣|大坂冬の陣]]では天満口を守備。翌年夏の陣では[[多田村|多田]]へ駐屯し、どさくさに紛れ[[塩川氏]]を滅ぼした。戦後は2300石の加増を受ける。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]7年（[[1621年]]）家督を嫡男[[能勢頼重|頼重]]に譲り隠居。
[[寛永]]3年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1626年]][[2月14日]]）[[江戸]]で没した。幕府は[[遺言]]によって領地をその子孫に分与した。長男[[能勢頼重]]に3,000石、次男[[能勢頼高]]に1,500石、三男[[能勢頼之（安土桃山時代）|能勢頼之]]に1,000石、四男[[能勢頼永]]に846石、五男[[能勢頼平]]300石をそれぞれに与えた。地黄城は頼重が引き継ぎ後の11代目[[能勢頼富]]まで続いた。
== 出典 == * 『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻第272

{{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|qq=}} | period = [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|qq=}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|qq=}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}*, Volume 272
{{translated page|ja|能勢頼次|version=100383704|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 1]]
}}

{{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | period = [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|qq=}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|qq=}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}*, Volume 272
{{translated page|ja|能勢頼次|version=100383704|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 1]]
}}

In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. '''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].

{{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | period = [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|qq=}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|qq=}}.
In [[Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}*, Volume 272
{{translated page|ja|能勢頼次|version=100383704|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Samurai of the Sengoku period]] [[Category:Hatamoto]] [[Category:1562 births]] [[Category:1626 deaths]]
[[Category:Completion tier 1]]
}}

Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|qq=}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|qq=}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].

Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].

{{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | period = [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|qq=}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|qq=}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}*, Volume 272

{{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | period = [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|qq=}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|qq=}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=}}*, Volume 272
{{translated page|ja|能勢頼次|version=100383704|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 1]]

{{Infobox person|birth_date=1562|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qid=Q205662}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|qid=Q11545923}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q52083736]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | period = [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) | death_date = [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] | allegiance = [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] → [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|qq=|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|qq=|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|qq=|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}*, Volume 272

In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|qq=|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|qq=|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].

'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].

Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}*, Volume 272
