In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].

'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}*, Volume 272

Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}*, Volume 272
{{translated page|ja|能勢頼次|version=100383704|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].

Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]] of the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and [[early Edo period]], serving as a [[:en:Hatamoto|Hatamoto]] of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Shogunate]], but during the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]] in the [[:en:Battle of Yamazaki|Battle of Yamazaki]], but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 19 (1614), during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]], Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in [[:en:Edo|Edo]]. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1562|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|WD=Q205662}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q52083736]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | period = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} 5 (1562) | death_date = [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto}} | allegiance = {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|WD=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide}} → {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} of the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} and [[early Edo period]], serving as a {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto}} of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Shogunate}}, but during the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}}, Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|WD=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide}} in the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|WD=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki}}, but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|WD=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara}} in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 19 (1614), during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|WD=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka}}, Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo}}. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}*, Volume 272
{{translated page|ja|能勢頼次|version=100383704|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1562|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|WD=Q205662}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | period = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} 5 (1562) | death_date = [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto}} | allegiance = {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|WD=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide}} → {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} of the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} and [[early Edo period]], serving as a {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto}} of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Shogunate}}, but during the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}}, Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|WD=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide}} in the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|WD=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki}}, but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|WD=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara}} in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|1=Noma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 19 (1614), during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|WD=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka}}, Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo}}. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}*, Volume 272
{{translated page|ja|能勢頼次|version=100383704|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:能勢頼次]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1562|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|WD=Q205662}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923|1=military commander}}}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2023}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Nose Yoritsugu | image = Nose Yoritugu.jpg | image_size = 250px | caption = Image of Nose Yoritsugu, owned by {{ill|Seifuku-ji|ja|清普寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifuku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | period = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} to [[early Edo period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 5 (1562) | death_date = [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3, [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] (February 14, 1626) | childhood_name = Takedomaru (竹童丸) | other_names = Sukejūrō, Sōemon | posthumous_name = Nikkō (日輝) | resting_place = [[Ikegami Honmon-ji]] | court_Rank = [[Governor of Iyo Province|Iyo-no-kami]], [[Governor of Settsu Province|Settsu-no-kami]] | shogunate = [[Tokugawa shogunate|Edo Shogunate]] {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}} | allegiance = {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|WD=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}} → {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} → [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] → {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} → [[Tokugawa Hidetada]] | clan = [[Nose clan|Nose clan]] | parents = Father: [[Nose Yoshiyuki]]; Mother: Daughter of [[Hatano Hidechika]] | siblings = [[Nose Yori Michi|Yorimichi]], [[Nose Yori Kōri|Yorigun]], '''Yoritsugu''', Kōngōin, wife of [[Awi Genshū]], wife of [[Inoue Ujitomo]], 2 unnamed sisters | spouse = Daughter of [[Inoue Yoshiyuki]] | children = '''[[Nose Yorishige|Yorishige]]''', [[Nose Yoritaka|Yoritaka]], [[Nose Yoriyuki|Yoriyuki]], [[Nose Yorinaga|Yorinaga]], [[Nose Yorihira|Yorihira]], daughter (wife of [[Tamura Akitō]] → wife of [[Matsudaira Masashige]]), [[Ōmi no Tsubone]], daughter (wife of [[Oda Takashige]]), ''adopted daughter (daughter of Matsudaira Masashige, wife of [[Ōta Shigenaga]])'' }}
'''Nose Yoritsugu''' (能勢 頼次) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} of the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} and [[early Edo period]], serving as a {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}} of the [[:en:Edo Shogunate|Edo Shogunate]].
== Life == Yoritsugu was born in {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 5 (1562) as the son of [[Nose Yoshiyuki]], a local lord of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}'s [[Nose District]]. The [[Nose clan]] had traditionally served the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga clan}}, but during the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, Yoritsugu’s two elder brothers perished in battles.
Yoritsugu sided with {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|WD=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}} in the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|WD=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki|1=Battle of Yamazaki}}, but following Mitsuhide's defeat, he fled and lost his lands to [[Shiokawa Nagamitsu]]. Though Yoritsugu served {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, his former territories were not restored. According to the *History of Nose Town* (*Nose Chōshi*), the lands in the Nose District were later allocated to [[Shimazu Yoshihiro]] as imperial compensation after the [[:en:Kyushu Campaign|Kyushu Campaign]].
After Hideyoshi's death, Yoritsugu aligned himself with {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 4 (1599) rumor that [[Ishida Mitsunari]] was plotting to attack Ieyasu, Yoritsugu, whose residence was adjacent to Mitsunari's, frequently reported his movements to Ieyasu. In the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|WD=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} in Keichō 5 (1600), Yoritsugu fought on Ieyasu's side, for which he was awarded 3,000 *koku* in the Nose District, as well as stewardship over an additional 6,800 *koku* in the same area. He began restoring the {{ill|Noma Shrine|ja|野間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noma Shrine|1=Q11646208|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646208}} and constructing {{ill|Jio Castle|ja|地黄城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jio Castle|1=Jiō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424750}}.
In {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 19 (1614), during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|WD=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka|1=Siege of Osaka}}, Yoritsugu guarded the Tenma district. In the following year, during the summer campaign, he stationed himself in [[Tada, Kawanishi|Tada]] and took advantage of the chaos to eliminate the [[Shiokawa clan]]. After the conflict, Yoritsugu was awarded an additional 2,300 *koku*. In {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} 7 (1621), he retired, passing on his role to his eldest son, [[Nose Yorishige]].
Yoritsugu died on [[1st month 18th day (lunar calendar)|1st month 18th day]] of [[:en:Kanei|Kanei]] 3 (February 14, 1626) in {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|WD=Q215646|lt=Edo|1=Edo}}. His will specified the division of his lands among his sons: 3,000 *koku* to his eldest son [[Nose Yorishige]], 1,500 *koku* to his second son [[Nose Yoritaka]], 1,000 *koku* to his third son [[Nose Yoriyuki]], 846 *koku* to his fourth son [[Nose Yorinaga]], and 300 *koku* to his fifth son [[Nose Yorihira]]. Jio Castle was inherited by Yorishige, and the Nose line continued through his descendants until the 11th generation [[Nose Yoritomi]].
== References == * *{{ill|Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|ja|寛政重修諸家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|1=Kansei Chōshū Shokafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457490}}*, Volume 272
